speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I will be even with thee, doubt it not.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: But why, why, why?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou hast forspoke my being in these wars

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And say’st it is not fit.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Well, is it, is it?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Is ’t not denounced against us? Why should not we

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Be there in person?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Well, I could reply:

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: If we should serve with horse and mares together,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The horse were merely lost. The mares would bear

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: A soldier and his horse.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What is ’t you say?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Your presence needs must puzzle Antony,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Take from his heart, take from his brain, from ’s time

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: What should not then be spared. He is already

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Traduced for levity, and ’tis said in Rome

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: That Photinus, an eunuch, and your maids

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Manage this war.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Sink Rome, and their tongues rot

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That speak against us! A charge we bear i’ th’ war,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And as the president of my kingdom will

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Appear there for a man. Speak not against it.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I will not stay behind.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Nay, I have done.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Here comes the Emperor.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Is it not strange, Canidius,

speaker: ANTONY
text: That from Tarentum and Brundusium

speaker: ANTONY
text: He could so quickly cut the Ionian Sea

speaker: ANTONY
text: And take in Toryne?—You have heard on ’t, sweet?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Celerity is never more admired

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Than by the negligent.

speaker: ANTONY
text: A good rebuke,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which might have well becomed the best of men,

speaker: ANTONY
text: To taunt at slackness.—Canidius, we will fight

speaker: ANTONY
text: With him by sea.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: By sea, what else?

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Why will

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: My lord do so?

speaker: ANTONY
text: For that he dares us to ’t.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: So hath my lord dared him to single fight.

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Ay, and to wage this battle at Pharsalia,

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Where Caesar fought with Pompey. But these offers,

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Which serve not for his vantage, he shakes off,

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: And so should you.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Your ships are not well manned,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Your mariners are muleteers, reapers, people

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Engrossed by swift impress. In Caesar’s fleet

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Are those that often have ’gainst Pompey fought.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Their ships are yare, yours heavy. No disgrace

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Shall fall you for refusing him at sea,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Being prepared for land.

speaker: ANTONY
text: By sea, by sea.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Most worthy sir, you therein throw away

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The absolute soldiership you have by land,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Distract your army, which doth most consist

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Of war-marked footmen, leave unexecuted

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Your own renownèd knowledge, quite forgo

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The way which promises assurance, and

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Give up yourself merely to chance and hazard

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: From firm security.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I’ll fight at sea.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I have sixty sails, Caesar none better.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Our overplus of shipping will we burn,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And with the rest full-manned, from th’ head of Actium

speaker: ANTONY
text: Beat th’ approaching Caesar. But if we fail,

speaker: ANTONY
text: We then can do ’t at land.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thy business?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The news is true, my lord; he is descried.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Caesar has taken Toryne.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Can he be there in person? ’Tis impossible;

speaker: ANTONY
text: Strange that his power should be. Canidius,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Our nineteen legions thou shalt hold by land,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And our twelve thousand horse. We’ll to our ship.—

speaker: ANTONY
text: Away, my Thetis.

speaker: ANTONY
text: How now, worthy soldier?

speaker: SOLDIER
text: O noble emperor, do not fight by sea!

speaker: SOLDIER
text: Trust not to rotten planks. Do you misdoubt

speaker: SOLDIER
text: This sword and these my wounds? Let th’ Egyptians

speaker: SOLDIER
text: And the Phoenicians go a-ducking. We

speaker: SOLDIER
text: Have used to conquer standing on the earth

speaker: SOLDIER
text: And fighting foot to foot.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Well, well, away.

speaker: SOLDIER
text: By Hercules, I think I am i’ th’ right.

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Soldier, thou art, but his whole action grows

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Not in the power on ’t. So our leader’s led,

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: And we are women’s men.

speaker: SOLDIER
text: You keep by land

speaker: SOLDIER
text: The legions and the horse whole, do you not?

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Marcus Octavius, Marcus Justeius,

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Publicola, and Caelius are for sea,

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: But we keep whole by land. This speed of Caesar’s

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Carries beyond belief.

speaker: SOLDIER
text: While he was yet in Rome,

speaker: SOLDIER
text: His power went out in such distractions as

speaker: SOLDIER
text: Beguiled all spies.

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Who’s his lieutenant, hear you?

speaker: SOLDIER
text: They say one Taurus.

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Well I know the man.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Emperor calls Canidius.

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: With news the time’s in labor, and throws forth

speaker: CANIDIUS
text: Each minute some.
